title: Engineered yeast breaks new record: a genome with over 50% synthetic DNA
description: Highly edited strain survives and replicates despite containing 7.5 artificial chromosomes.  Highly edited strain survives and replicates despite containing 7.5 artificial chromosomes.
bodyText:
Access to this article via Konsortium Baden Wuerttemberg vertreten Albert Ludwigs Universität Freiburg is not available. Biologists have produced a strain of yeast whose genome is more than 50% synthetic DNA. Standard brewer’s yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) stores its genetic blueprint across 16 chromosomes. In the new strain, 6.5 of those chromosomes were edited and synthesized in the laboratory — and an extra one was stitched together from edited bits of the yeast’s genetic code.
